rulings.Case Title: RANDY TREAS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Exxon Mobil Corporations Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Delay In Prosecution
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, August 11, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Exxon Mobil Corporation¿s motion to dismiss for delay in prosecution is granted. No opposition filed. Hearing required unless counsel for the moving party notifies Mr. Treas of the tentative ruling and he says that he does not wish to contest. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LEVY HUGHES JR VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, AUGUST 11, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 2. Defendant Truitt & White Lumber Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternative motion for summary judgment is denied as to negligence, strict liability and substantial factor causation and off calendar as to false representation, premises owner/contractor liability and punitive damages. As to negligence, strict liability and substantial factor causation, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant, and that such exposure was a substantial factor. Plaintiff¿s responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories and his deposition testimony preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Moreover, defendant did not present any evidence that it propounded written contention discovery seeking the names of all persons with knowledge and all documents relating to plaintiff¿s allegation that asbestos attributable to defendant was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to plaintiff¿s asbestos-related illness. Plaintiff represents that he did not allege premises owner/contractor liability against defendant. Plaintiff dismissed his claim for false representation and request for punitive damages. (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE EGGLETON et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, AUGUST 11, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3. Defendant Getinge USA, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is off calendar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Eggleton was exposed to asbestos-containing oven insulation attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs¿ supplemental responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories, Mr. Schluntz¿s deposition testimony and defendant¿s responses to General Order 129 interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Even if defendant had sustained its burden, Mr. Schluntz¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether Mr. Eggleton was exposed to asbestos-containing oven insulation attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Schluntz¿s declaration are overruled. Plaintiffs dismissed their claims for false representation and punitive damages, which were the only issues that defendant sought to be summarily adjudicated. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: VICTOR PAILLET VS. CROWN CORK & SEAL COMPANY, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, AUGUST 11, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 7. Defendant Lehigh Hanson, Inc.¿s motion to quash service of summons: hearing required so that the court can review the ¿information obtained, under seal, by plaintiffs¿ attorneys in Casey¿ to determine if there is any reasonable basis for plaintiff¿s apparent contention that defendant is the alter ego of Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc. If there is such a reasonable basis, then the hearing on this motion will be continued to December 13 to permit plaintiff to take jurisdictional discovery and the plaintiff may file supplemental opposition papers no later than November 30 and defendant may file supplemental reply papers no later than December 7. = (220/HEK)
